title: Starship’s path to reusability looks murky after SpaceX’s S-1
summary: SpaceX's recent IPO and Starship rocket test flight delivered two big data points that offer a realistic vision for the coming years — and one that may disappoint both the company's boosters and its critics.

title: SpaceX’s Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO
summary: American Airlines said Tuesday it plans to install Starlink on more than 500 Airbus aircraft, the latest carrier win for IPO-bound SpaceX.

title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
summary: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
summary: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training data that AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire.

title: Dutch government blocks US company from acquisition, citing ‘risk to public interest’
summary: The move to block the acquisition of the cloud company that hosts the Dutch digital ID service comes as Europe continues to reduce its reliance on U.S. technology.

title: Ghost hackers: the cybersecurity mystery that nobody has solved
summary: A shadowy group that stole and dumped the NSA’s most powerful hacking tools still has implications for how companies think about digital risk today.

title: Iranian hackers blamed for breach of Los Angeles transit system that took weeks to recover
summary: An Israeli cybersecurity firm said Iran’s government is behind Ababil of Minab, a fake hacktivist persona that has claimed a series of data breaches after the start of the war in Iran.
tags: Los Angeles County Metropolitan Transportation Authority

title: Ferrari’s first EV is not for you
summary: The Ferrari Luce seems to be more aimed at regulatory compliance and China, putting a lot of pressure on the Jony Ive-designed EV.

title: Universal Music Group and TikTok renew agreement to combat unauthorized AI music
summary: For years, UMG has pushed platforms, streaming services, and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies.

title: Spotify now lets you stream narrated magazine articles, too
summary: The streaming service said that, starting today, there will be over 650 long-form magazine articles (available in English only) from publications like Rolling Stone, The Atlantic, Vogue, Variety, Billboard, Vibe, GQ, Wired, Vanity Fair, and Pitchfork.

title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket rates end May 29
summary: Save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass before prices increase on May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to join the tech epicenter in San Francisco.

title: 7-Eleven data breach affects over 185,000 people’s personal data
summary: The data breach included names, dates of birth, postal addresses, and Social Security numbers, according to a state government listing.

title: Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord raises $250M at $3B valuation
summary: Stord offers a network of physical warehouses and inventory management software for e-commerce. It bills itself as a sort of anti-Amazon, giving brands "the speed to compete" while still owning their customer relationships.

title: Musk says US military suicide drones used Starlink in violation of SpaceX rules
summary: Musk says drones used Starlink instead of Starshield, blames military contractor.

title: NASA takes steps toward building Moon Base, including discussing a "perimeter"
summary: "We also obviously want to be very mindful of the Outer Space Treaty."

title: We're starting to see some PC makers respond to Apple's MacBook Neo
summary: Sub-$600 laptops have existed for years, but consistently good ones remain rare.

title: Millions of AI agents imperiled by critical vulnerability in open source package
summary: "BadHost" was found in Starlette, a package with 325 million weekly downloads.

title: Want an oxygen-rich atmosphere? Stuff oxygen’s friends in the mantle.
summary: Getting carbon and sulfur into Earth’s interior may be part of oxygen’s story.

title: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
summary: A creepy saved post on Instagram linked man to AI porn account, FBI says.

title: 3D-printable humanoid legs let robotics experiments run wild
